surface_score_policies.claim_boundary: Task-scoped assistant evidence from the versioned benchmark mix. Compare only within this score version and use-case surface; this is not a global intelligence score.
Task-scoped coding evidence from the versioned benchmark mix. It does not imply repository-editing or general software-engineering capability.
Task-scoped reasoning evidence from the versioned benchmark mix. It is not an expert-reasoning, global intelligence, or leaderboard claim.

coverage_expansion_priorities.why: This is the highest-leverage answer loop for fast assistant recommendations under common Mac memory budgets.
coverage_expansion_priorities.what_it_would_change: Replace static duration and quant-fidelity caveats with observed speed, memory, and same-family quality-retention evidence.

coverage_expansion_priorities.why: A reviewed Qwen3 baseline adds a current cross-generation assistant comparison on common Apple Silicon memory budgets.
coverage_expansion_priorities.what_it_would_change: Let recommendations compare observed Qwen3 speed, memory, and assistant retention with Qwen2.5 while recording Qwen3 perplexity only for future same-family quant comparisons.

coverage_expansion_priorities.why: A reviewed Qwen3.5 baseline adds a recent-model assistant comparison on Apple Silicon after exact artifact and local llama.cpp compatibility verification.
coverage_expansion_priorities.what_it_would_change: Let recommendations compare observed Qwen3.5 speed, memory, task time, and assistant retention with Qwen3 and Qwen2.5 while keeping perplexity comparisons within each model family.

coverage_expansion_priorities.why: Coding recommendations need executable reference evidence before they can distinguish fast setup fit from useful coding behavior.
coverage_expansion_priorities.what_it_would_change: Promote coding answers from thin local/static evidence toward sampled reference evidence while keeping repo-edit and gold claims out of scope.

coverage_expansion_priorities.why: Reasoning answers should show when the evidence is only a thin exact-answer sample versus sampled MMLU-Pro reference evidence.
coverage_expansion_priorities.what_it_would_change: Give Recommend a direct next benchmark when reasoning evidence is missing, thin, or unstable.

coverage_expansion_priorities.why: Windows/NVIDIA is the core second tier, but it cannot influence recommendations until one full loop is proven on hardware.
coverage_expansion_priorities.what_it_would_change: Move Windows/NVIDIA from preflight-only preview toward a bounded technical beta for one known-good GGUF path.

benchmark_maturity_levels.gate_summary: Roadmap metadata only. The benchmark is not runnable until a fixture or dataset policy, harness, scoring policy, and claim boundary exist.
Runnable only for small pinned local fixtures with deterministic scoring and explicit thin-sample caveats.
Candidate for stronger local evidence after broader samples, repeatability, and observed duration or failure-rate metadata are reviewed.
Recognized or justified reference benchmark candidate that still needs a complete reproducible harness, scoring, dataset, or sandbox review.
Runnable intentionally selected reference evidence with pinned dataset or fixture revision, preserved raw/scoring artifacts, and explicit claim boundaries.
Potential high-legitimacy evidence that requires legal/access controls, stronger sandbox/runtime controls, and maintainer review before runnable product claims.
Runnable gold evidence only after reference gates, dataset/legal/access gates, sandbox controls, and maintainer-review gates are all satisfied.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Decision-sized instruction-following sample; not a leaderboard protocol.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Instruction-following decision evidence only; not broad assistant quality or leaderboard evidence.
promotion_blockers: Pin exact sample/revision policy before promoting to reference evidence.
Replace estimated duration and token-volume metadata with observed bands.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Five pinned synthetic transcript-retention cases for quick local setup guidance.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Thin local assistant sample; useful for setup guidance, not a global assistant or reference claim.
promotion_blockers: Broader assistant task family coverage is required before stronger local evidence.
Repeated-run variance and observed duration metadata are not yet required.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Three pinned static repair prompts with deterministic constraint scoring and no generated-code execution.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Thin local coding sample; not HumanEval+, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, or executable code-correctness evidence.
promotion_blockers: Executable coding evidence needs sandboxed unit-test execution.
Broader coding samples and repeated-run reporting are required before stronger local evidence.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Three pinned synthetic exact-answer cases for quick local reasoning setup guidance.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Thin local reasoning sample; not GPQA, MMLU-Pro, expert reasoning, reference, or gold evidence.
promotion_blockers: Larger exact-answer sample and leakage-reviewed source policy are needed before stronger local evidence.
Reference reasoning requires recognized benchmark controls.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Pinned MMLU-Pro snapshot with canary, sampled local reference, and deeper/full reference limits.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Sampled MMLU-Pro reference evidence; useful as a broad knowledge/reasoning reference signal, not a leaderboard or gold claim.
promotion_blockers: Observed duration and token-volume metadata are needed for stronger local recommendation copy.
Observed local runs are needed to calibrate duration and failure behavior.
Leaderboard-style claims require the full official protocol and separate publication controls.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Pinned EvalPlus HumanEval+ revision with canary and full local reference limits.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: EvalPlus HumanEval+ reference coding evidence under pass@1 unit tests; not repo-edit, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, gold, or broad agentic coding proof.
promotion_blockers: Observed duration and token-volume metadata are needed for stronger local recommendation copy.
Observed local runs are needed to calibrate duration and failure behavior.
Gold or leaderboard-style claims require separate publication controls and maintainer review.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Pinned EvalPlus MBPP+ revision with canary, sampled local reference, and full local reference limits.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: EvalPlus MBPP+ reference coding breadth evidence under pass@1 unit tests; not repo-edit, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, gold, or broad agentic coding proof.
promotion_blockers: Observed duration and token-volume metadata are needed for stronger local recommendation copy.
Observed local runs are needed to calibrate duration and failure behavior.
Gold or leaderboard-style claims require separate publication controls and maintainer review.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Pinned InferGrade quant-fidelity corpus under infergrade_perplexity_v1; directly comparable only when model family, checkpoint, tokenizer, corpus, and protocol match.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Same-family quant-fidelity reference evidence only; perplexity does not prove assistant, coding, reasoning, cross-family quality, or general model capability.
promotion_blockers: Observed duration and token-volume metadata are needed across real contributed local runs.
Reference precision baselines are needed before Hub can present quality-retention deltas.
Gold or leaderboard-style quant claims require deeper corpus controls and maintainer review.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Short local deployment profile for TTFT, load time, and output speed.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Deployment fitness evidence only; speed does not prove answer quality.
promotion_blockers: Observed hardware/runtime duration bands are needed before stronger local comparison claims.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Throughput-oriented local deployment profile for offline generation.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Deployment throughput evidence only; not task capability or quant fidelity.
promotion_blockers: Observed duration, variance, and hardware normalization metadata are needed for stronger comparison copy.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Longer-context local deployment profile for latency and memory behavior.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Long-context deployment fitness evidence only; not long-context reasoning quality.
promotion_blockers: Observed memory and variance metadata are needed before stronger local comparison copy.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Future local preference-style reference candidate only after judge policy and prompt set are reviewed.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Future preference-style assistant reference candidate; not a default local lane or leaderboard-style result.
promotion_blockers: Define deterministic or reviewed judge policy.
Pin prompt fixtures and isolate subjective scoring limitations.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Future tiny pinned repo-edit fixtures with rollback-safe execution and deterministic assertions.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Future local repo-edit smoke only; not SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, or broad agentic coding evidence.
promotion_blockers: Define fixture repositories and rollback policy.
Design sandboxed patch application and deterministic scoring.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Synthetic shape-only fixtures until local accepted dataset snapshots and leakage controls are implemented.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Future hard reasoning reference candidate; not runnable and not gold evidence.
promotion_blockers: No committed restricted data.
Define access-gated local dataset flow and leakage-safe artifacts.
Pin dataset revision after access controls exist.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Future pinned task-window pass@1 generation mode before broader LiveCodeBench modes.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Future coding reference candidate; not SWE-bench, not repo-edit proof, and not a default local lane.
promotion_blockers: Pin task window and upstream version.
Implement sandbox, timeout, and failure-state preservation.

benchmark_status_matrix.sample_policy: Future tiny pinned SWE-bench Verified subset with maintainer review and sandboxed environment metadata.
benchmark_status_matrix.claim_boundary: Future gold evidence candidate only; not runnable, not default laptop evidence, and not a public leaderboard claim.
promotion_blockers: Define legal/access and task-subset policy.
Implement sandboxed environment prep and maintainer-review workflow.
Block product copy from presenting this as self-run local default evidence.

score_policies.description: Use IFEval prompt-level strict accuracy as the primary assistant instruction-following score.
Score short assistant transcripts by deterministic retention of corrected facts, output constraints, and user preferences.
Use exact answer-letter accuracy for sampled multiple-choice reference checks.

score_policies.display_name: Strict answer-letter accuracy with empty-think normalization
score_policies.description: Use exact answer-letter accuracy after removing only a leading empty thinking wrapper and trailing runtime terminal markers.
Score compact local reasoning prompts with deterministic exact-answer matching.

score_policies.display_name: Coding primary pass@1 with normalized completions
score_policies.description: Use EvalPlus pass@1+ after versioned code-only prompting, unambiguous fence extraction, HumanEval body conversion, and fail-closed scorer validation. HumanEval+ is the core decision signal; MBPP+ adds breadth.
Score small local coding outputs with deterministic static constraints and explicit malformed-output failure states.
Expose latency and throughput metrics as deployment evidence without folding them into task capability score.
Use lower perplexity as a supplemental quant-fidelity signal for nearby quantized variants.

benchmark_scopes.description: Short, local-friendly checks intended to help choose a quantized setup for a concrete use case.
Deeper, heavier checks intended for stronger comparison evidence after the first decision path works.

evidence_lanes.description: Minimal execution evidence that proves a setup can run one small path without supporting quality conclusions.
evidence_lanes.claim_boundary: Useful for confirming the setup runs. Not enough for model quality, recommendation, or leaderboard-style claims.

evidence_lanes.description: Short, local-friendly evidence that can help choose which quantized setup to try on this hardware and use case.
evidence_lanes.claim_boundary: Good for choosing a practical next setup. Not enough by itself for leaderboard-style model quality claims.

evidence_lanes.description: Broader or heavier evidence that strengthens comparisons after the first local decision path works.
evidence_lanes.claim_boundary: Useful for comparing nearby variants and families when decision evidence is too thin.

evidence_lanes.description: High-legitimacy evidence that needs curated datasets, stronger reproducibility controls, or heavier execution.
evidence_lanes.claim_boundary: Best for trusted public claims, but usually too expensive or operationally sensitive for a default laptop run.

capability_surfaces.description: Instruction-following, structured-output, conversational retention, and assistant behavior evidence from pinned local tasks.
capability_surfaces.claim_boundary: Useful for a local assistant setup choice when paired with clear task counts and caveats. Not a global assistant ranking.

capability_surfaces.description: Coding generation, repair, structured patch, and bounded repo-edit evidence from deterministic local tasks.
capability_surfaces.claim_boundary: Useful for coding-oriented setup choices. Not a SWE-bench or agentic software engineering claim unless gold gates are met.

capability_surfaces.description: Exact-answer, multiple-choice, or structured reasoning checks that can run locally under pinned fixture rules.
capability_surfaces.claim_boundary: Useful as a narrow reasoning signal. Not an expert reasoning or restricted-dataset claim without reference or gold controls.

capability_surfaces.description: Perplexity or reference-output evidence for nearby quantized artifacts.
capability_surfaces.claim_boundary: Useful for quant-to-quant comparison. Not a general capability score.

capability_surfaces.description: Runtime, latency, throughput, memory, and stability evidence for a local setup.
capability_surfaces.claim_boundary: Useful for whether a setup fits the hardware and interaction pattern. Not proof that the model answers well.

suites.description: Assistant-style prompting, instruction adherence, and the latency signals people use to judge everyday chat quality.
Coding completions, practical editing behavior, and the longer interactive prompts that matter for local coding assistants.
Exact-answer reasoning plus a sampled broad-knowledge reference signal for local setup decisions.
Reference-text perplexity that helps compare nearby quantized variants when task scores cluster tightly.

benchmark_groups.description: Run IFEval to check whether the model follows detailed textual instructions cleanly.
Run short multi-turn transcript checks for remembered facts, corrections, and output constraints.
Run a sampled MMLU-Pro reference pass for harder broad knowledge and reasoning coverage.
Run small native exact-answer reasoning prompts scored by deterministic answer matching.
Run small native coding repair prompts scored by deterministic static output constraints.
Run HumanEval+ for the most important pass@1 coding signal.
Add MBPP+ for broader everyday coding coverage beyond the core set.
Measure TTFT, load time, and output speed for interactive requests.
Measure throughput-oriented generation behavior under a small batch load.
Measure latency and memory pressure under longer prompts.
Run the shared perplexity reference pass for quant-to-quant comparisons.

checks.description: Short transcript-retention benchmark for corrected facts, remembered preferences, and output constraints.
checks.selection_guidance: Adds a quick local signal for whether an assistant retains facts and corrections across short transcript turns.

checks.description: Sampled harder broad knowledge and reasoning benchmark.
checks.selection_guidance: Adds a sampled reference signal for broad knowledge and reasoning. It is stronger than a quick decision check but not a leaderboard claim.

checks.description: Small local reasoning prompts scored by deterministic exact-answer matching.
checks.selection_guidance: Adds a quick native reasoning signal for exact-answer tasks. It is a thin local sample and not a broad reasoning benchmark.

checks.description: Small local coding repair prompts scored by deterministic static constraints without executing generated code.
checks.selection_guidance: Adds a quick native coding signal for structured repair output. It avoids unsafe execution and is a thin local sample.

checks.description: Core coding benchmark for code generation quality.
Broader coding benchmark focused on practical Python tasks.
Interactive deployment profile for TTFT, load, and output speed.
Batch deployment profile for throughput-oriented comparisons.
Deployment profile for longer-context latency and memory behavior.
Same-family quant fidelity signal based on a pinned InferGrade reference corpus.

shortcuts.description: Fast assistant evidence with one capability benchmark and one deployment profile.
Coding-focused evidence with HumanEval+ and the most useful deployment checks.
A broader assistant comparison with chat, batch, and quant fidelity signals.
Stronger assistant evidence with sampled MMLU-Pro, instruction following, chat memory, deployment, and quant fidelity signals.

planned_benchmark_candidates.value: Adds preference-style assistant quality evidence for models that look similar on instruction following.
planned_benchmark_candidates.cost: medium token volume; requires careful prompt and judge policy design
planned_benchmark_candidates.why_not_default: Needs an explicit judge policy before it can be trusted as a local decision score.

planned_benchmark_candidates.value: Adds a small local coding-agent edit task before broader SWE-style suites.
planned_benchmark_candidates.cost: small-to-medium token volume; needs deterministic fixture repos
planned_benchmark_candidates.why_not_default: Needs deterministic fixture repositories and rollback-safe execution before default inclusion.

planned_benchmark_candidates.value: Adds hard expert-level reasoning coverage that helps distinguish models with similar instruction-following scores.
planned_benchmark_candidates.cost: medium-to-large token volume; likely reference-only for local users
planned_benchmark_candidates.why_not_default: Access is gated and the benchmark is intentionally difficult and reference-only; it should not run without explicit user consent and leakage-safe handling.
planned_benchmark_candidates.access_requirements: Users must accept the Hugging Face dataset conditions before files can be accessed.
planned_benchmark_candidates.dataset_handling_policy: Do not commit, log, screenshot, or publish GPQA examples in plain text; fixtures must use synthetic non-GPQA examples until access and leakage controls are implemented.
planned_benchmark_candidates.fixture_policy: Synthetic shape-only fixtures only until the harness can load an accepted local dataset snapshot without exposing real examples.
planned_benchmark_candidates.harness_prerequisite: Add a local dataset path/token flow, pinned revision metadata, and redaction-safe artifacts before making this runnable.

planned_benchmark_candidates.value: Adds contemporary coding task coverage beyond static HumanEval+/MBPP+ style checks.
planned_benchmark_candidates.cost: large token volume plus sandboxed execution; needs task-set pinning
planned_benchmark_candidates.why_not_default: Needs pinned task windows and robust local sandboxing before it can support reproducible local comparisons.

planned_benchmark_candidates.value: Adds high-legitimacy software-engineering task evidence for the strongest coding setups.
planned_benchmark_candidates.cost: very large token volume and long wall-clock runtime; likely cloud or maintainer-reviewed gold lane first
planned_benchmark_candidates.why_not_default: Operationally expensive enough that it should start as maintainer-reviewed gold evidence, not a default laptop run.
